versets.trad: that I may do righteousness in what I left behind.” No! It is but a word he utters; and behind them is a barrier until the Day they are raised.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it will be said to them: why not the tenth of a tenth of this before this? Repentance at the hour of death is but a “word of the tongue” (kalima) without reality. The barzakh — the barrier between the two worlds — forbids all return: “return before the road is closed”, but the road has closed. The time of work was life; afterward there is only the waiting.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح / ت ر ك
mots.sens: I will do good in what I left

mots.racine: و ر ي / ب ر ز خ
mots.def: barzakh: isthmus, barrier between two states.

versets.ar: فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ فَلَا أَنسَابَ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَلَا يَتَسَاءَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-idhā nufikha fī-ṣ-ṣūri fa-lā ansāba baynahum yawmaʾidhin wa-lā yatasāʾalūna
versets.trad: When the Trumpet is blown, on that Day there will be no kinship between them, nor will they question one another.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: on that Day the bonds of kinship avail no more, the secondary causes are severed, regret is vain; each meets the fruit of what he has committed. He whose balance is weighed down with goods bears its adornment; he whose disgrace appears has every kind of trial — the fire licks their faces, the traces betray them, the argument falls upon them: no answer heard, no excuse received, no chastisement lifted.

mots.racine: ن ف خ / ص و ر
mots.def: nafakha: to blow; ṣūr: trumpet, horn.

mots.racine: ن س ب

versets.ar: فَمَن ثَقُلَتْ مَوَازِينُهُ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: fa-man thaqulat mawāzīnuhu fa-ulāʾika humu-l-mufliḥūna
versets.trad: Those whose scales are heavy — those are the prosperous,
versets.commentaire: The sūra that opened on “aflaḥa-l-muʾminūn” finds here again the mufliḥūn: those whose balance tips toward the good are the triumphant. In Qushayrī's perspective, the falāḥ promised at the start is verified at the end: he whose balance is weighed down with goods bears its adornment. The success announced to the humble believer is realized at the weighing of works.

mots.racine: ث ق ل / و ز ن
mots.def: thaqula: to weigh heavy; mawāzīn: scales.

mots.racine: ف ل ح

versets.ar: وَمَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوَازِينُهُ فَأُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ فِي جَهَنَّمَ خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man khaffat mawāzīnuhu fa-ulāʾika-lladhīna khasirū anfusahum fī jahannama khālidūna
versets.trad: and those whose scales are light — those have lost their souls, abiding in Hell forever.
versets.commentaire: The counterpoint of the preceding verse: the light balance marks the loss of self. Qushayrī makes of this “loss of the soul” (khasira anfusahum) the height of disaster: not to lose an outward good, but to lose oneself, eternally. The lightness of the balance is but the reflection of the emptiness of a life that gathered nothing for the return.

mots.racine: خ ف ف / و ز ن
خ س ر / ن ف س
mots.def: khasira: to lose; nafs: soul, self.

versets.ar: تَلْفَحُ وُجُوهَهُمُ النَّارُ وَهُمْ فِيهَا كَالِحُونَ
versets.tr: talfaḥu wujūhahumu-n-nāru wa-hum fīhā kāliḥūna
versets.trad: The Fire will scorch their faces, and therein they will grimace.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, commenting on the Day of Judgment, already described: the fire licks their faces, and the traces of their state can be read upon them. The “grimacing” face (kāliḥ) — lips drawn back baring the teeth — is the image of despair graven in the flesh. The chastisement becomes a face, manifesting outwardly the inner ruin of one who has lost his soul.

mots.racine: ل ف ح / و ج ه / ن و ر
mots.def: lafaḥa: to scorch, to singe; wujūh: faces.

mots.racine: ك ل ح
mots.def: kāliḥ: with grimacing face, lips drawn back.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَكُنْ آيَاتِي تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَكُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam takun āyātī tutlā ʿalaykum fa-kuntum bihā tukadhdhibūna
versets.trad: Were not My signs recited to you, and you used to deny them?
versets.commentaire: The reproach of the Day: My signs were recited to you, and you denied them. In Qushayrī's logic, this is the exhausting of all excuse — the proof was given, the reminder was made. The reproach awaits no answer: it establishes the guilt of one who heard the Truth and held it for a lie. The past recitation becomes the indictment.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ت ل و
mots.sens: My signs were recited to you

mots.racine: ك ذ ب
mots.def: kadhdhaba: to hold for a lie.

versets.ar: قَالُوا رَبَّنَا غَلَبَتْ عَلَيْنَا شِقْوَتُنَا وَكُنَّا قَوْمًا ضَالِّينَ
versets.tr: qālū rabbanā ghalabat ʿalaynā shiqwatunā wa-kunnā qawman ḍāllīna
versets.trad: They will say: “Our Lord, our wretchedness overcame us, and we were a straying people!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they have uttered the truth — but on a day when the admission no longer avails, when the excuse is no longer received. To acknowledge “our wretchedness overcame us, we were astray” is a truth told too late. The time of useful acknowledgment was life; on the Day of reckoning, lucidity only deepens the torment of what could have been avoided.

mots.racine: غ ل ب / ش ق و
mots.def: ghalaba: to overcome; shiqwa: wretchedness, misfortune.

mots.racine: ض ل ل

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا أَخْرِجْنَا مِنْهَا فَإِنْ عُدْنَا فَإِنَّا ظَالِمُونَ
versets.tr: rabbanā akhrijnā minhā fa-in ʿudnā fa-innā ẓālimūna
versets.trad: Our Lord, bring us out of it; and if we return, then we shall be wrongdoers!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real says: were they sent back, they would return to what they were forbidden. He knows their return to this world will not be, but He knows too how it would be were it to be. Their promise — “if we return, we shall be wrongdoers” — is hollow: the divine knowledge knows they would relapse. The supplication arrives after the decree is sealed.

mots.racine: خ ر ج
ع و د / ظ ل م
mots.sens: if we relapse, we shall be unjust

versets.ar: قَالَ اخْسَئُوا فِيهَا وَلَا تُكَلِّمُونِ
versets.tr: qāla-khsaʾū fīhā wa-lā tukallimūni
versets.trad: He will say: “Be gone into it, and do not speak to Me!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives this verse a striking depth: then their trial is consummated, their pain deepens — for so long as they remembered God, the separation was not total; but when they are cut off from His remembrance, the misery is complete. And he relates that some men of God almost envy this reproach: they say: “Would that He addressed us! Does He not so speak to us? The blame of the Beloved is sweeter than the praise of strangers.”

mots.racine: خ س أ
mots.def: khasaʾa: to drive off with scorn (as a dog).

mots.racine: ك ل م
mots.sens: and do not speak to Me

versets.ar: إِنَّهُ كَانَ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْ عِبَادِي يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
versets.tr: innahu kāna farīqun min ʿibādī yaqūlūna rabbanā āmannā fa-ghfir lanā wa-rḥamnā wa-anta khayru-r-rāḥimīna
versets.trad: There was a group of My servants who used to say: “Our Lord, we believe! Forgive us and have mercy on us! You are the best of the merciful!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real takes vengeance on His enemies with what gladdens the hearts of His friends — such is the Real's quarrel. He says: there was a group of My friends who proclaimed My praise and My laud, and you made them an object of your mockery; today I reward them, and take vengeance on whoever scorned them. The prayer of these servants — “we believe, forgive us” — was their only weapon, and it triumphs.

mots.racine: ف ر ق / ع ب د
غ ف ر / ر ح م
mots.sens: forgive us and have mercy on us
mots.def: ghafara: to forgive; raḥima: to have mercy.

versets.ar: فَاتَّخَذْتُمُوهُمْ سِخْرِيًّا حَتَّىٰ أَنسَوْكُمْ ذِكْرِي وَكُنتُم مِّنْهُمْ تَضْحَكُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ttakhadhtumūhum sikhriyyan ḥattā ansawkum dhikrī wa-kuntum minhum taḍḥakūna
versets.trad: But you took them in mockery until they made you forget My remembrance, and you used to laugh at them.
versets.commentaire: The grievance turned against the mockers: by dint of mocking the believers, they forgot the remembrance of God. In Qushayrī's perspective, the mockery of God's friends is mockery of God Himself: it turns the heart from dhikr and absorbs it in vain laughter. The chastisement weds the fault — they who laughed will be forever deprived of joy.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / س خ ر
ن س ي / ذ ك ر
mots.sens: until they made you forget My remembrance
mots.def: ansā: to make forget; dhikr: remembrance.

versets.ar: إِنِّي جَزَيْتُهُمُ الْيَوْمَ بِمَا صَبَرُوا أَنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْفَائِزُونَ
versets.tr: innī jazaytuhumu-l-yawma bimā ṣabarū annahum humu-l-fāʾizūna
versets.trad: Indeed, today I have rewarded them for their patience: they are the triumphant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: today I reward them, and take vengeance on whoever crossed them. The patience (ṣabr) of the believers before the mockeries finds here its crowning: they are the “triumphant” (fāʾizūn). The verb in the past — “I have rewarded them” — voices the accomplished certitude: their victory is no longer promised but realized, the fruit of silent endurance in the trial.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ص ب ر
mots.sens: I rewarded them today for their patience
mots.def: jazā: to requite; ṣabara: to be patient.

mots.racine: ف و ز

versets.ar: قَالَ كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ عَدَدَ سِنِينَ
versets.tr: qāla kam labithtum fī-l-arḍi ʿadada sinīna
versets.trad: He will say: “How many years did you tarry on the earth?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the number of years of things, however great, shrinks before what surpasses it without end. The divine question — how many years? — measures the earthly sojourn against eternity. What seemed long to man proves minute; a whole life is but a breath before what awaits, joy or torment without end.

mots.racine: ل ب ث / أ ر ض
mots.sens: how long did you tarry on earth?

mots.racine: ع د د / س ن و

versets.ar: قَالُوا لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ فَاسْأَلِ الْعَادِّينَ
versets.tr: qālū labithnā yawman aw baʿḍa yawmin fa-sʾali-l-ʿāddīna
versets.trad: They will say: “We tarried a day, or part of a day; ask those who keep count!”
versets.commentaire: Their answer confirms the lesson: “a day, or part of a day.” In Qushayrī's spirit, the time lived shrinks before eternity to the point of seeming almost nothing. This admission of brevity is itself a judgment: they squandered an instant for an endless chastisement. The awareness of time lost awakens when it is too late to redeem it.

mots.racine: ل ب ث / ي و م
mots.sens: we tarried a day or part of a day

mots.racine: س أ ل / ع د د

versets.ar: قَالَ إِن لَّبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا لَّوْ أَنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qāla in labithtum illā qalīlan law annakum kuntum taʿlamūna
versets.trad: He will say: “You tarried but a little, had you only known!
versets.commentaire: God confirms: “but a little.” Qushayrī: the length of their sojourn under the earth, if they were in repose, shrinks before the reposes of the Resurrection; and if they were pains, they vanish before what they see that Day of the successive chastisements. The “had you only known” is the regret of knowledge come too late, when the time of work is past.

mots.racine: ل ب ث / ق ل ل
ع ل م

versets.ar: أَفَحَسِبْتُمْ أَنَّمَا خَلَقْنَاكُمْ عَبَثًا وَأَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْنَا لَا تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-ḥasibtum annamā khalaqnākum ʿabathan wa-annakum ilaynā lā turjaʿūna
versets.trad: Did you think that We created you in vain, and that you would not be returned to Us?”
versets.commentaire: Key verse of the sūra. Qushayrī: ʿabath is sport, play, busying oneself with what distracts from the Real; God has commanded this to no servant, called none to it, urged none toward it. The one who plays acts outside the way of rectitude... Yet the Real is holy and transcendent: He does nothing in sport and commands nothing vain. Man is not created for nothing: he bears a meaning and a return, and to deny this return would be to make of God a player.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ع ب ث
ر ج ع
mots.sens: you would not be returned to Us

versets.ar: فَتَعَالَى اللَّهُ الْمَلِكُ الْحَقُّ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْكَرِيمِ
versets.tr: fa-taʿālā-llāhu-l-maliku-l-ḥaqqu lā ilāha illā huwa rabbu-l-ʿarshi-l-karīmi
versets.trad: Exalted be God, the King, the Truth! There is no god but He, Lord of the noble Throne!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real, by the attributes of His majesty, is one; in the glory of His eternity and the loftiness of His qualities, He is alone. His essence is truth, His attributes are truth, His word is veracious; no creature has a right upon Him, and none of His favors toward His servants is owed. And on “the noble Throne”: it is not the Throne that adorns Him, but the Throne that is honored in that He has joined it to Himself by a privileged joining; the karīm is the beautiful, and generosity is the negation of all baseness.

mots.racine: م ل ك / ح ق ق
mots.def: malik: king, sovereign; ḥaqq: true, real.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ر ش / ك ر م

versets.ar: وَمَن يَدْعُ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا آخَرَ لَا بُرْهَانَ لَهُ بِهِ فَإِنَّمَا حِسَابُهُ عِندَ رَبِّهِ إِنَّهُ لَا يُفْلِحُ الْكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man yadʿu maʿa-llāhi ilāhan ākhara lā burhāna lahu bihi fa-innamā ḥisābuhu ʿinda rabbihi innahu lā yufliḥu-l-kāfirūna
versets.trad: And whoever calls upon another god alongside God, having no proof of it, his reckoning is only with his Lord. Truly the disbelievers do not prosper!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: his reckoning returns to God for his term, and his chastisement comes to him from God in the present moment; it is the ignorance deposited in his heart that made him consent to worship another beside Him. And on their pretext — “we worship them only that they bring us nearer to God”: it is a word without proof of reason or witness of tradition, pure lie and slander that no argument sustains. The sūra that opened on falāḥ closes on “lā yufliḥu”: no triumph for the denier.

mots.racine: د ع و / أ ل ه
mots.sens: calls upon another god beside God
mots.def: daʿā: to call upon; ilāh: deity.

mots.racine: ف ل ح / ك ف ر

versets.ar: وَقُل رَّبِّ اغْفِرْ وَارْحَمْ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qul rabbi-ghfir wa-rḥam wa-anta khayru-r-rāḥimīna
versets.trad: And say: “My Lord, forgive and have mercy! You are the best of the merciful!”
versets.commentaire: The sūra is sealed on a supplication. Qushayrī: forgive the sins, veil the faults, lavish Your gifts; and have mercy, so that the assaults of division and the trials that descend may not gain power over us. The mercy sought in invocation is one of the kinds of grace. After the portrait of the believers, the creation of man, the history of the prophets and the denial of the deniers, all converges toward this final recourse to the Best of the merciful.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / غ ف ر / ر ح م
mots.def: ghafara: to forgive; raḥima: to have mercy.

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ر ح م
